title: The 11 Best Cybersecurity Software for Small Business
subtitle: A ranked analysis of endpoint protection platforms (EPP) and extended detection and response (XDR) tools tailored for small to medium-sized business needs and budgets.
audience: Small business IT buyers choosing endpoint protection and security tools

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Threat Detection & Response (30%), Ease of Use (25%), Pricing & Scalability (20%), Compliance & Reporting (10%), Support (10%), and Integration Ecosystem (5%).

answer_capsule: The best cybersecurity software for small businesses is Bitdefender GravityZone, followed by CrowdStrike Falcon Go and SentinelOne Control.

criteria.name: Threat Detection & Response
criteria.description: Effectiveness in preventing, detecting, and responding to malware, ransomware, phishing, and zero-day exploits, based on independent lab tests (AV-Test, AV-Comparatives) and feature sets (EDR/XDR capabilities).

criteria.name: Ease of Use & Management
criteria.description: Simplicity of the management console, ease of deployment, clarity of alerts, and automation features. Crucial for SMBs with limited or no dedicated IT security staff.

criteria.name: Pricing & Scalability
criteria.description: Overall value for money, transparent pricing models, and the ability to scale protection from a few endpoints to hundreds without significant cost or complexity increases.

criteria.name: Compliance & Reporting
criteria.description: Availability of features and reports that help businesses meet regulatory compliance standards like GDPR, HIPAA, and PCI DSS.

criteria.name: Support & Documentation
criteria.description: Quality and accessibility of customer support (24/7, phone, chat), knowledge base, and community forums.

criteria.name: Integration Ecosystem
criteria.description: Ability to integrate with other common SMB tools, such as RMM software, PSA platforms, and cloud services like Microsoft 365.

query_intents: managed detection and response for smb

guide.q: Understanding the Cybersecurity Landscape for SMBs
guide.a: Small businesses are prime targets for cyberattacks because they often have valuable data without the enterprise-grade defenses of larger companies. Modern cybersecurity software goes beyond traditional antivirus, offering layered protection including endpoint detection and response (EDR), which actively monitors for and responds to suspicious behavior, not just known viruses.

Look for a solution with a centralized management console, strong anti-ransomware capabilities (including rollback features), web and email filtering, and automated threat response. For businesses in regulated industries, robust reporting and compliance features are non-negotiable.

how_to_choose: Assess Your Team's Expertise: If you don't have a dedicated IT security person, prioritize solutions known for their ease of use and strong customer support.

Consider Your Environment: Ensure the software effectively protects all your devices, whether they are Windows, macOS, servers, or mobile, and integrates with your key cloud applications like Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace.

Request a Trial and a Quote: Never buy based on a feature list alone. Most providers offer a free trial. Use it to test deployment and management. Always get a custom quote, as list prices can be misleading.

faqs.q: What is the difference between antivirus and a full cybersecurity suite?
faqs.a: Traditional antivirus primarily uses signature-based detection to block known malware. A modern cybersecurity suite, often called an Endpoint Protection Platform (EPP), adds multiple layers of defense, including behavioral analysis, machine learning to detect new (zero-day) threats, firewalls, web filtering, and often Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) for threat hunting and incident response.

faqs.q: How much should a small business expect to spend on cybersecurity software?
faqs.a: Pricing is typically per-device (endpoint), per-year. For a quality SMB solution, expect to pay between $30 to $80 per endpoint annually. Costs can increase for advanced features like managed detection and response (MDR) services.

faqs.q: Do I need a dedicated IT person to manage this software?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. Most solutions on this list are designed with SMBs in mind and feature cloud-based consoles that simplify management. However, interpreting alerts and responding to complex threats still requires some level of technical knowledge. For businesses with no IT staff, a managed service (like our wildcard pick, Huntress) can be a better fit.

faqs.q: Can I use free antivirus software for my business?
faqs.a: It is strongly discouraged. Free antivirus products typically lack centralized management, advanced threat protection, dedicated support, and their licensing agreements often prohibit commercial use. The investment in a proper business-grade solution is a critical cost of doing business.

honest_disclosures: Most candidates are established endpoint protection platforms (EPP/EDR); the list has less coverage of emerging areas like cloud security posture management (CSPM) for SMBs.
Pricing for business software is often opaque and requires a direct sales quote. The pricing bands provided are estimates based on publicly available data and typical SMB discounts.

glossary.definition: A cybersecurity technology that continuously monitors and collects endpoint data, using automated rules and analytics to detect, investigate, and respond to advanced threats.

entries.name: Bitdefender
entries.best_for: Small businesses seeking the best overall balance of top-tier threat detection, performance, and value for money.
entries.verdict: Bitdefender GravityZone Business Security earns the top spot by consistently delivering elite-level threat protection, validated by independent labs, in a package that is both affordable and manageable for small businesses.
entries.verdict_short: Elite protection that's affordable and manageable for SMBs without dedicated security teams.
entries.praise: Its layered next-gen security, including machine learning and anti-exploit technology, stops even the most advanced threats with minimal impact on system performance.
entries.praise_short: Top-tier detection rates with low system performance impact.
entries.criticism: The GravityZone console, while powerful, can present a steeper learning curve for complete beginners compared to some simpler competitors.
entries.criticism_short: Management console can be complex for absolute beginners.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: CrowdStrike
entries.best_for: Tech-forward small businesses that want enterprise-grade, AI-powered threat hunting and response capabilities and are willing to pay a premium.
entries.verdict: CrowdStrike Falcon Go packages its market-leading EDR technology for small businesses, offering unparalleled visibility and AI-driven protection against sophisticated attacks.
entries.verdict_short: Top-tier, AI-powered EDR technology scaled down for SMB consumption.
entries.praise: The platform's threat intelligence and real-time response capabilities are second to none, providing a level of security previously out of reach for most SMBs.
entries.criticism: It is one of the most expensive options in the SMB market, and its feature set can be overkill for businesses with very basic security needs.
entries.criticism_short: Premium pricing and potential feature overkill for some.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: SentinelOne
entries.best_for: SMBs wanting fully autonomous protection that can automatically remediate threats, including rolling back ransomware attacks, with minimal human intervention.
entries.verdict: SentinelOne Control stands out for its powerful AI-driven, autonomous platform that not only detects but also actively responds to threats in real-time, making it ideal for lean IT teams.
entries.verdict_short: Powerful, autonomous AI platform that actively remediates threats for lean IT teams.
entries.praise: Its ability to rollback a device to a pre-attack state is a game-changer for ransomware recovery, saving businesses critical time and money.
entries.criticism: While highly automated, the console can sometimes be less intuitive for policy creation and reporting compared to platforms with a longer history in the SMB space.
entries.criticism_short: Console can be less intuitive for policy management.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Sophos
entries.best_for: Businesses looking for a unified security platform that combines endpoint, firewall, email, and mobile protection in a single, easy-to-manage ecosystem.
entries.verdict: Sophos Intercept X excels by offering a comprehensive, synchronized security system where different products (like endpoint and firewall) share intelligence to provide a more coordinated defense.
entries.verdict_short: A comprehensive, synchronized system where security products share intelligence for better defense.
entries.praise: The Sophos Central management console is widely praised for its clean interface and for making it simple to manage multiple security products from one place.
entries.criticism: The endpoint agent can sometimes be heavier on system resources compared to more lightweight competitors, particularly on older hardware.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Trend Micro
entries.best_for: Small businesses that need a reliable, set-and-forget security solution with a long track record of effective threat protection.
entries.verdict: Trend Micro's Worry-Free Business Security lives up to its name by providing robust, multi-layered protection that is straightforward to deploy and requires minimal ongoing administration.
entries.verdict_short: Robust, multi-layered protection that's simple to deploy and requires minimal administration.
entries.praise: It offers a comprehensive feature set for the price, including protection for email, web, and mobile devices, all managed from a single console.
entries.criticism: The user interface, while functional, feels dated compared to the more modern designs of newer competitors in the EDR space.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: ESET
entries.best_for: Organizations that prioritize a very low system performance impact without compromising on solid, multi-layered threat detection.
entries.verdict: ESET PROTECT provides highly effective and reliable security with one of the lightest endpoint agents in the industry, making it an excellent choice for businesses with older hardware or performance-sensitive applications.
entries.verdict_short: Highly effective security with a famously lightweight agent, ideal for older hardware.
entries.praise: Its UEFI scanner is a key differentiator, capable of detecting threats that hide in a system's firmware before the main operating system even loads.
entries.praise_short: Unique UEFI scanner detects pre-boot threats.
entries.criticism: The management console is powerful but can feel overly granular and complex for simple tasks, requiring more clicks than some competitors.
entries.criticism_short: Management console can be overly granular.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Malwarebytes
entries.best_for: Small businesses and non-profits looking for a very user-friendly platform with strong remediation capabilities to clean up already-infected systems.
entries.best_for_short: Best for ease of use & cleanup
entries.verdict: Malwarebytes for Business leverages its renowned malware removal prowess into a full endpoint protection suite that is exceptionally easy to deploy and manage, even for non-technical users.
entries.verdict_short: Renowned malware removal in an easy-to-use endpoint protection suite for non-technical users.
entries.praise: The Nebula cloud platform is incredibly intuitive, making setup and monitoring a breeze for small teams without dedicated IT staff.
entries.criticism: While its detection is strong, it lacks some of the advanced EDR and threat hunting features found in higher-ranked, enterprise-focused competitors.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks advanced EDR and threat hunting features.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Webroot (OpenText)
entries.best_for: Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and their SMB clients who need an extremely fast, lightweight agent that won't slow down endpoints.
entries.verdict: Webroot Business Endpoint Protection, now part of OpenText, is a favorite in the MSP channel due to its incredibly small and fast agent, which installs in seconds and has a negligible performance impact.
entries.verdict_short: An MSP favorite for its tiny, fast agent with negligible performance impact.
entries.praise: The cloud-based management console is built for multi-tenancy, making it highly efficient for MSPs to manage hundreds of clients from a single dashboard.
entries.criticism: Its reliance on a cloud-based threat database means its offline protection capabilities are not as robust as some competitors that use more advanced local heuristics.
entries.criticism_short: Offline protection is less robust than competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Avast (Gen Digital)
entries.best_for: Very small businesses or startups on a tight budget needing basic, reliable endpoint protection that is simple to manage.
entries.verdict: Avast Business Antivirus provides dependable, no-frills security at a highly competitive price point, making it an accessible entry-level option for businesses prioritizing affordability.
entries.verdict_short: Dependable, no-frills security at a very competitive price for budget-focused businesses.
entries.praise: The Business Hub management console is clean and straightforward, allowing for easy deployment and monitoring of devices without requiring deep technical expertise.
entries.criticism: Lacks the sophisticated EDR, threat intelligence, and compliance features necessary for businesses with more complex security needs or regulatory requirements.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks advanced EDR and compliance features.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Acronis
entries.best_for: Businesses seeking an integrated solution that combines cybersecurity, data backup, and disaster recovery into a single platform.
entries.verdict: Acronis Cyber Protect offers a unique value proposition by tightly integrating its robust backup and recovery technology with a solid endpoint protection suite, simplifying vendor management for SMBs.
entries.verdict_short: Uniquely integrates robust backup and recovery with endpoint protection, simplifying vendor management.
entries.praise: Having anti-ransomware protection work hand-in-hand with automated backups provides a powerful, multi-layered defense and recovery strategy.
entries.praise_short: Powerful synergy of anti-ransomware and backups.
entries.criticism: While the integration is excellent, its core cybersecurity features are not as advanced or proven in independent tests as the top-tier, security-only focused providers.
entries.criticism_short: Core security features lag behind focused EDR leaders.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.

entries.name: Huntress
entries.best_for: SMBs without any internal IT security staff who need a fully managed service that provides 24/7 threat hunting and expert remediation.
entries.verdict: Huntress is a wildcard because it's not just software; it's a Managed Detection and Response (MDR) service that layers human threat hunters on top of technology to find and eliminate persistent threats that automated tools miss.
entries.verdict_short: A managed service with human experts who actively hunt for threats automated tools miss.
entries.praise: It provides access to an elite security operations center (SOC) team for a fraction of the cost of hiring one in-house, making high-end security accessible to small businesses.
entries.praise_short: Affordable access to an elite SOC team.
entries.criticism: It is designed to augment, not replace, an existing antivirus/EPP solution, meaning it represents an additional cost and layer of management (though minimal).
entries.criticism_short: Augments existing antivirus, adding another cost layer.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-04.
